title: jmap: Message/set deletedAt patch has no ownership check — any message can be soft-deleted
description:
## Bug

In daemon/src/jmap.rs message_set(), the 'deletedAt' patch path (line ~2344) calls store.soft_delete_message(msg_id, false) with no check that the caller is the message sender.

The 'destroy' path (line ~2395-2418) correctly fetches the message and compares rows[0].sender_id != account_id before deleting. The 'body' patch correctly uses edit_message_body() which has an internal ownership check (EditBodyResult::Forbidden).

But 'deletedAt' has no such guard: any authenticated user can soft-delete any message in the system by sending a Message/set update patch with {'deletedAt': '<timestamp>'}.

## Impact

An authenticated daemon user can soft-delete chat messages they did not author, effectively silencing other users.

## Fix

Before calling soft_delete_message(), check that the message sender_id matches the caller's account_id (same pattern as the destroy path). Add a store method that combines the ownership check and the soft-delete atomically, or reuse the same fetch + check pattern.
